NAME

main.c - 🖥️ Server main entry point: multi-client connection manager with per-client rendering threads (60fps video + 172fps audio)

Thread-safe keyboard queue (lock-free SPSC ring buffer).

int server_main (void)
Server mode entry point for unified binary.

mixer_t *volatile g_audio_mixer = NULL
Global audio mixer instance for multi-client audio processing. h265_server_context_t *volatile g_h265_server = NULL
Global H.265 codec server context. static_cond_t g_shutdown_cond = STATIC_COND_INIT
Global shutdown condition variable for waking blocked threads. rate_limiter_t * g_rate_limiter = NULL
Global rate limiter for connection attempts and packet processing. bool g_server_encryption_enabled = false
Global flag indicating if server encryption is enabled. private_key_t g_server_private_key = {0}
Global server private key (first identity key, for backward compatibility). private_key_t g_server_identity_keys [MAX_IDENTITY_KEYS] = {0}
Global server identity keys array (multi-key support). size_t g_num_server_identity_keys = 0
Number of loaded server identity keys. public_key_t g_client_whitelist [MAX_CLIENTS] = {0}
Global client public key whitelist. size_t g_num_whitelisted_clients = 0
Number of whitelisted clients.

Detailed Description

🖥️ Server main entry point: multi-client connection manager with per-client rendering threads (60fps video + 172fps audio)

MODULAR COMPONENTS:

  • main.c (this file): Server initialization, signal handling, connection management
  • client.c: Per-client lifecycle, threading, and state management
  • protocol.c: Network packet processing and protocol implementation
  • stream.c: Video mixing, ASCII frame generation, and caching
  • render.c: Per-client rendering threads with rate limiting
  • stats.c: Performance monitoring and resource tracking

CONCURRENCY MODEL:

The server creates multiple thread types per client:

1.
Receive thread: Handles incoming packets from client (protocol.c functions)
2.
Send thread: Manages outgoing packet delivery (client.c)
3.
Video render thread: Generates ASCII frames at 60fps (render.c)
4.
Audio render thread: Mixes audio streams at 172fps (render.c)
5.
Stats logger thread: Periodic performance reporting (stats.c)

Thread Safety:

  • Lock ordering: Always acquire g_client_manager_rwlock BEFORE per-client mutexes
  • Snapshot pattern: Copy client state under mutex, then process without locks
  • Signal-safe shutdown: SIGINT handler only sets flags and closes sockets
  • Deterministic cleanup: Main thread waits for all worker threads before exit

WHY THE REFACTORING:

The original server.c became unmaintainable at 2408+ lines, making it:

  • Too large for LLM context windows (limited AI-assisted development)
  • Difficult for humans to navigate and understand
  • Slow to compile and modify
  • Hard to isolate bugs and add new features

The modular design enables:

  • Faster development cycles (smaller compilation units)
  • Better IDE support (jump-to-definition, IntelliSense)
  • Easier testing and debugging (isolated components)
  • Future extensibility (new protocols, renderers, optimizations)

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

September 2025

Version

2.0 (Post-Modularization)

Definition in file server/main.c.

Function Documentation

int server_main (void )

Server mode entry point for unified binary. This function implements the complete server lifecycle including:

  • Server-specific initialization (crypto, shutdown callback)
  • Network socket setup and binding
  • Main connection accept loop
  • Client lifecycle management
  • Graceful shutdown and cleanup

Options are already parsed by the main dispatcher before this function is called, so they are available via global opt_* variables.

Returns

0 on success, non-zero error code on failure

Example

# Invoked by dispatcher after options are parsed:
ascii-chat server --port 8080
# Options parsed in main.c, then server_main() called

Definition at line 1781 of file server/main.c.

1781                       {
1782   // Common initialization (options, logging, lock debugging) now happens in main.c before dispatch
1783   // This function focuses on server-specific initialization
1784 
1785   // Register atomic for debug tracking
1786   ATOMIC_REGISTER_AUTO(g_should_exit, NULL);
1787 
1788   // Register shutdown check callback for library code
1789   shutdown_register_callback(check_shutdown);
1790 
1791   // Initialize status screen log buffer if:
1792   // 1. Terminal is interactive AND status_screen is enabled, OR

1794   // In non-interactive mode without explicit flag, logs go directly to stdout/stderr
1795   if ((GET_OPTION(status_screen) && terminal_is_interactive()) ||
1796       (GET_OPTION(status_screen_explicitly_set) && GET_OPTION(status_screen))) {
1797     ui_status_log_init();
1798 
1799     // Initialize interactive grep for status screen
1800     log_search_init();

1806     // Print detailed error context if available
1807     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Crypto initialization failed");
1808     FATAL(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Crypto initialization failed");
1809   }
1810   log_debug("Crypto initialized successfully");
1811 
1812   // Handle keepawake: check for mutual exclusivity and apply mode default
1813   // Server default: keepawake DISABLED (use --keepawake to enable)
1814   if (GET_OPTION(enable_keepawake) && GET_OPTION(disable_keepawake)) {
1815     FATAL(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "--keepawake and --no-keepawake are mutually exclusive");
1816   }
1817   if (GET_OPTION(enable_keepawake)) {
1818     (void)platform_enable_keepawake();

1820 
1821   log_info("ascii-chat server starting...");
1822 
1823   // log_info("SERVER: Options initialized, using log file: %s", log_filename);
1824   int port = GET_OPTION(port);

1826     log_error("Invalid port configuration: %d", port);
1827     FATAL(ERROR_CONFIG, "Invalid port configuration: %d", port);
1828   }
1829 
1830   ascii_simd_init();
1831   precalc_rgb_palettes(weight_red, weight_green, weight_blue);
1832 
1833   // Simple signal handling (temporarily disable complex threading signal handling)
1834   log_debug("Setting up simple signal handlers...");
1835 
1836   // Handle Ctrl+C for cleanup
1837   platform_signal(SIGINT, server_handle_sigint);
1838   // Handle termination signal (SIGTERM is defined with limited support on Windows)
1839   platform_signal(SIGTERM, server_handle_sigterm);
1840 #ifndef _WIN32
1841   // SIGPIPE not supported on Windows
1842   platform_signal(SIGPIPE, SIG_IGN);
1843   // Note: SIGUSR1 is registered in src/main.c for all modes
1844 #endif
1845 
1846 #ifndef NDEBUG
1847   // Lock debug thread is now started in src/main.c (all modes)
1848   // Initialize statistics system
1849   if (stats_init() != 0) {
1850     FATAL(ERROR_THREAD, "Statistics system initialization failed");
1851   }
1852 #endif
1853 
1854   // Create background worker thread pool for server operations
1855   g_server_worker_pool = thread_pool_create("server_workers");
1856   if (!g_server_worker_pool) {
1857     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create server worker thread pool");
1858     FATAL(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create server worker thread pool");
1859   }
1860 
1861   // Spawn statistics logging thread in worker pool

1863     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Statistics logger thread creation failed");
1864   } else {
1865     log_debug("Statistics logger thread started");
1866   }
1867 
1868   // Network setup - Use tcp_server abstraction for dual-stack IPv4/IPv6 binding
1869   log_debug("Config check: GET_OPTION(address)='%s', GET_OPTION(address6)='%s'", GET_OPTION(address),
1870             GET_OPTION(address6));
1871 
1872   bool ipv4_has_value = (strlen(GET_OPTION(address)) > 0);
1873   bool ipv6_has_value = (strlen(GET_OPTION(address6)) > 0);
1874   // Check if address is localhost (any loopback address, not just exact default)
1875   bool ipv4_is_default = is_localhost_ipv4(GET_OPTION(address));
1876   bool ipv6_is_default = is_localhost_ipv6(GET_OPTION(address6));
1877 
1878   log_debug("Binding decision: ipv4_has_value=%d, ipv6_has_value=%d, ipv4_is_default=%d, ipv6_is_default=%d",
1879             ipv4_has_value, ipv6_has_value, ipv4_is_default, ipv6_is_default);
1880 
1881   // Determine bind configuration
1882   bool bind_ipv4 = false;
1883   bool bind_ipv6 = false;
1884   const char *ipv4_address = NULL;
1885   const char *ipv6_address = NULL;
1886 
1887   if (ipv4_has_value && ipv6_has_value && ipv4_is_default && ipv6_is_default) {
1888     // Both are defaults: dual-stack with default localhost addresses
1889     bind_ipv4 = true;
1890     bind_ipv6 = true;
1891     ipv4_address = "127.0.0.1";
1892     ipv6_address = "::1";
1893     log_info("Default dual-stack: binding to 127.0.0.1 (IPv4) and ::1 (IPv6)");
1894   } else if (ipv4_has_value && !ipv4_is_default && (!ipv6_has_value || ipv6_is_default)) {
1895     // IPv4 explicitly set, IPv6 is default or empty: bind only IPv4
1896     bind_ipv4 = true;
1897     bind_ipv6 = false;
1898     ipv4_address = GET_OPTION(address);
1899     log_info("Binding only to IPv4 address: %s", ipv4_address);
1900   } else if (ipv6_has_value && !ipv6_is_default && (ipv4_is_default || !ipv4_has_value)) {
1901     // IPv6 explicitly set, IPv4 is default or empty: bind only IPv6
1902     bind_ipv4 = false;
1903     bind_ipv6 = true;
1904     ipv6_address = GET_OPTION(address6);
1905     log_info("Binding only to IPv6 address: %s", ipv6_address);
1906   } else {
1907     // Both explicitly set or one explicit + one default: dual-stack
1908     bind_ipv4 = true;
1909     bind_ipv6 = true;
1910     ipv4_address = ipv4_has_value ? GET_OPTION(address) : "127.0.0.1";
1911     ipv6_address = ipv6_has_value ? GET_OPTION(address6) : "::1";
1912     log_info("Dual-stack binding: IPv4=%s, IPv6=%s", ipv4_address, ipv6_address);
1913   }
1914

1917   // Create H.265 server decoder context for multi-client codec support

1919   if (!h265_server_ctx) {

1924   server_context_t server_ctx = {

1930       .audio_mixer = g_audio_mixer,

1932       .stats_mutex = &g_stats_mutex,

1941   // Configure TCP server
1942   tcp_server_config_t tcp_config = {
1943       .port = port,
1944       .ipv4_address = ipv4_address,
1945       .ipv6_address = ipv6_address,
1946       .bind_ipv4 = bind_ipv4,

1955   // Initialize TCP server (creates and binds sockets)
1956   memset(&g_tcp_server, 0, sizeof(g_tcp_server));
1957   asciichat_error_t tcp_init_result = tcp_server_init(&g_tcp_server, &tcp_config);
1958   if (tcp_init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1959     // Signal shutdown to allow threads to exit before we call FATAL
1960     atomic_store_bool(&g_should_exit, true);
1961     log_debug("TCP server init failed, signaling shutdown to threads");
1962 
1963     // Clean up worker thread pool if it was created
1964     if (g_server_worker_pool) {
1965       log_debug("Early cleanup: destroying server worker pool");
1966       thread_pool_destroy(g_server_worker_pool);
1967       g_server_worker_pool = NULL;
1968     }
1969 
1970     FATAL(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize TCP server");
1971   }
1972 
1973   // Initialize WebSocket server (for browser clients)

1975       .port = GET_OPTION(websocket_port),

1981 
1982   // Log WSS configuration if provided

1986   }

1991     log_warn("Failed to initialize WebSocket server - browser clients will not be supported");
1992   } else {
1993     log_info("WebSocket server initialized on port %d", GET_OPTION(websocket_port));
1994   }
1995 
1996   // DEBUG: Print lock state immediately after WebSocket init

1998   // debug_sync_print_state();  // Disabled: causes AddressSanitizer stack-use-after-return crash
1999 
2000   // =========================================================================
2001   // UPnP Port Mapping (Quick Win for Direct TCP)
2002   // =========================================================================
2003   // Track UPnP success for ACDS session type decision
2004   // If UPnP fails, we need to create a WebRTC session to enable client connectivity
2005   bool upnp_succeeded = false;
2006 
2007   // Try to open port via UPnP so direct TCP works for ~70% of home users.
2008   // If this fails, clients fall back to WebRTC automatically - not fatal.
2009   //
2010   // Strategy:
2011   //   1. UPnP (works on ~90% of home routers)
2012   //   2. NAT-PMP fallback (Apple routers)
2013   //   3. If both fail: use ACDS + WebRTC (reliable, but slightly higher latency)
2014   if (GET_OPTION(enable_upnp)) {
2015     asciichat_error_t upnp_result = nat_upnp_open(port, "ascii-chat Server", &g_upnp_ctx);
2016 
2017     if (upnp_result == ASCIICHAT_OK && g_upnp_ctx) {
2018       char public_addr[22];
2019       if (nat_upnp_get_address(g_upnp_ctx, public_addr, sizeof(public_addr)) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2020         char msg[256];
2021         safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "🌐 Public endpoint: %s (direct TCP)", public_addr);
2022         log_console(LOG_INFO, msg);
2023         log_info("UPnP: Port mapping successful, public endpoint: %s", public_addr);
2024         upnp_succeeded = true;
2025       }
2026     } else {
2027       log_info("UPnP: Port mapping unavailable or failed - will use WebRTC fallback");
2028       log_console(LOG_INFO, "📡 Clients behind strict NATs will use WebRTC fallback");
2029     }
2030   } else {
2031     log_debug("UPnP: Disabled (use --upnp to enable automatic port mapping)");
2032   }
2033 
2034   // Initialize synchronization primitives
2035   if (rwlock_init(&g_client_manager_rwlock, "clients") != 0) {
2036     FATAL(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to initialize client manager rwlock");
2037   }
2038 
2039   // Register global sync primitives for debugging
2040   NAMED_REGISTER_RWLOCK(&g_client_manager_rwlock, "client_manager_rwlock", NULL);
2041 
2042   // Lock debug system already initialized earlier in main()
2043 
2044   // Check if SIGINT/SIGTERM was received during initialization
2045   // If so, skip the accept loop entirely and go to cleanup
2046   if (atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2047     log_info("Shutdown signal received during initialization, skipping server startup");
2048     goto cleanup;
2049   }
2050 
2051   // Lock debug thread already started earlier in main()
2052 
2053   // NOTE: g_client_manager is already zero-initialized in client.c with = {0}
2054   // We only need to initialize the mutex
2055   mutex_init(&g_client_manager.mutex, "client_manager");
2056 
2057   // Initialize uthash head pointer for O(1) lookup (uthash requires NULL initialization)
2058   g_client_manager.clients_by_id = NULL;
2059 
2060   // Initialize connection rate limiter (prevents DoS attacks)
2061   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2062     log_debug("Initializing connection rate limiter...");
2063     g_rate_limiter = rate_limiter_create_memory();
2064     if (!g_rate_limiter) {
2065       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to initialize rate limiter");
2066       if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2067         FATAL(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create connection rate limiter");
2068       }
2069     } else {
2070       log_info("Connection rate limiter initialized (50 connections/min per IP)");
2071     }
2072   }
2073 
2074   // Initialize audio mixer (always enabled on server)
2075   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2076     log_debug("Initializing audio mixer for per-client audio rendering...");
2077     g_audio_mixer = mixer_create(MAX_CLIENTS, AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE);
2078     if (!g_audio_mixer) {
2079       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to initialize audio mixer");
2080       if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2081         FATAL(ERROR_AUDIO, "Failed to initialize audio mixer");
2082       }
2083     } else {
2084       if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2085         log_debug("Audio mixer initialized successfully for per-client audio rendering");
2086       }
2087     }
2088   }
2089 
2090   // Initialize H.265 codec server context (always enabled on server)
2091   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2092     log_debug("Initializing H.265 codec server context...");
2093     g_h265_server = h265_server_context_create();
2094     if (!g_h265_server) {
2095       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to initialize H.265 codec context");
2096       if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2097         FATAL(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "Failed to initialize H.265 codec context");
2098       }
2099     } else {
2100       if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2101         log_debug("H.265 codec server context initialized successfully");
2102       }
2103     }
2104   }
2105 
2106   // Initialize mDNS context for LAN service discovery (optional)
2107   // mDNS allows clients on the LAN to discover this server without knowing its IP
2108   // Can be disabled with --no-mdns-advertise
2109   // Note: Actual advertisement is deferred until after ACDS session creation (if --acds is enabled)
2110   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && !GET_OPTION(no_mdns_advertise)) {
2111     log_debug("Initializing mDNS for LAN service discovery...");
2112     g_mdns_ctx = asciichat_mdns_init();
2113     if (!g_mdns_ctx) {
2114       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to initialize mDNS (non-fatal, LAN discovery disabled)");
2115       log_warn("mDNS disabled - LAN service discovery will not be available");
2116       g_mdns_ctx = NULL;
2117     } else {
2118       log_debug("mDNS context initialized, advertisement deferred until session string is ready");
2119     }
2120   } else if (GET_OPTION(no_mdns_advertise)) {
2121     log_info("mDNS service advertisement disabled via --no-mdns-advertise");
2122   }
2123 
2124   // ========================================================================
2125   // Session Host Creation (for discovery mode support)
2126   // ========================================================================
2127   // Create session_host to track clients in a transport-agnostic way.
2128   // This enables future discovery mode where participants can become hosts.
2129   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2130     session_host_config_t host_config = {
2131         .port = port,
2132         .ipv4_address = ipv4_address,
2133         .ipv6_address = ipv6_address,
2134         .max_clients = GET_OPTION(max_clients),
2135         .encryption_enabled = g_server_encryption_enabled,
2136         .key_path = GET_OPTION(encrypt_key),
2137         .password = GET_OPTION(password),
2138         .callbacks = {0}, // No callbacks for now
2139         .user_data = NULL,
2140     };
2141 
2142     server_ctx.session_host = session_host_create(&host_config);
2143     if (!server_ctx.session_host) {
2144       // Non-fatal: session_host is optional, server can work without it
2145       log_warn("Failed to create session_host (discovery mode support disabled)");
2146     } else {
2147       log_debug("Session host created for discovery mode support");
2148     }
2149   }
2150 
2151   // ========================================================================
2152   // MAIN CONNECTION LOOP - Delegated to tcp_server
2153   // ========================================================================
2154   //
2155   // The tcp_server module handles:
2156   // 1. Dual-stack IPv4/IPv6 accept loop with select() timeout
2157   // 2. Spawning client_handler threads for each connection
2158   // 3. Responsive shutdown when g_tcp_server.running is set to false
2159   //
2160   // Client lifecycle is managed by ascii_chat_client_handler() which:
2161   // - Performs rate limiting
2162   // - Calls add_client() to initialize structures and spawn workers
2163   // - Blocks until client disconnects
2164   // - Calls remove_client() to cleanup and stop worker threads
2165 
2166   // ACDS Session Creation: Register this server with discovery service
2167   // This also determines the session string for mDNS (if --acds is enabled)
2168   char session_string[64] = {0};
2169   bool session_is_mdns_only = false;
2170 
2171   // ACDS Registration (conditional on --discovery flag)
2172   if (GET_OPTION(discovery)) {
2173     // Security Requirement Check (Issue #239):
2174     // Server IP must be protected by password, identity verification, or explicit opt-in
2175 
2176     // Auto-detection: Check if password or identity verification is configured
2177     const char *password = GET_OPTION(password);
2178     bool has_password = password && strlen(password) > 0;
2179     const char *encrypt_key = GET_OPTION(encrypt_key);
2180     bool has_identity = encrypt_key && strlen(encrypt_key) > 0;
2181     bool explicit_expose = GET_OPTION(discovery_expose_ip) != 0;
2182 
2183     // Validate security configuration BEFORE attempting ACDS connection
2184     bool acds_expose_ip_flag = false;
2185 
2186     if (has_password || has_identity) {
2187       // Auto-enable privacy: IP revealed only after verification
2188       acds_expose_ip_flag = false;
2189       log_plain("🔒 ACDS privacy enabled: IP disclosed only after %s verification",
2190                 has_password ? "password" : "identity");
2191     } else if (explicit_expose) {
2192       // Explicit opt-in to public IP disclosure
2193       // Only prompt if running interactively (can prompt user)
2194       // When non-interactive (automated/scripted), treat explicit flag as confirmation
2195       bool is_interactive = terminal_can_prompt_user();
2196 
2197       if (is_interactive) {
2198         log_warn("WARNING: You are about to allow PUBLIC IP disclosure!\n"
2199                  "Anyone with the session string will be able to see your IP address.\n"
2200                  "This is NOT RECOMMENDED unless you understand the privacy implications.");
2201 
2202         if (!platform_prompt_yes_no("Do you want to proceed with public IP disclosure", false)) {
2203           log_info("IP disclosure not confirmed. Server will run WITHOUT discovery service.");
2204           goto skip_acds_session;
2205         }
2206       }
2207 
2208       // User confirmed (or running non-interactively with explicit flag) - proceed with public IP disclosure
2209       acds_expose_ip_flag = true;
2210       log_info("Public IP disclosure CONFIRMED.\n"
2211                "Your IP address will be visible to anyone with the session string");
2212     } else {
2213       // Security violation: No password, no identity, no explicit opt-in
2214       log_error("Cannot create ACDS session: No security configured!\n"
2215                 "You must either:\n"
2216                 "1. Set a password: --password \"your-secret\"\n"
2217                 "2. Use identity key: --key ~/.ssh/id_ed25519\n"
2218                 "3. Explicitly allow public IP: --acds-expose-ip (NOT RECOMMENDED)\n"
2219                 "\n"
2220                 "Server will run WITHOUT discovery service.");
2221       goto skip_acds_session;
2222     }
2223 
2224     // Security is configured, proceed with ACDS connection
2225     const char *acds_server = GET_OPTION(discovery_server);
2226     uint16_t acds_port = (uint16_t)GET_OPTION(discovery_port);
2227 
2228     log_info("Attempting to create session on ACDS server at %s:%d...", acds_server, acds_port);
2229 
2230     acds_client_config_t acds_config;
2231     acds_client_config_init_defaults(&acds_config);
2232     SAFE_STRNCPY(acds_config.server_address, acds_server, sizeof(acds_config.server_address));
2233     acds_config.server_port = acds_port;
2234     acds_config.timeout_ms = 5 * MS_PER_SEC_INT;
2235 
2236     // Allocate ACDS client on heap for server lifecycle
2237     g_acds_client = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acds_client_t), acds_client_t *);
2238     if (!g_acds_client) {
2239       log_error("Failed to allocate ACDS client");
2240       goto skip_acds_session;
2241     }
2242 
2243     asciichat_error_t acds_connect_result = acds_client_connect(g_acds_client, &acds_config);
2244     if (acds_connect_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2245       log_error("Failed to connect to ACDS server at %s:%d: %s", acds_server, acds_port,
2246                 asciichat_error_string(acds_connect_result));
2247       goto skip_acds_session;
2248     }
2249     if (acds_connect_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2250       // Prepare session creation parameters
2251       acds_session_create_params_t create_params;
2252       memset(&create_params, 0, sizeof(create_params));
2253 
2254       // Use server's Ed25519 identity public key if available
2255       if (g_server_encryption_enabled && has_identity) {
2256         memcpy(create_params.identity_pubkey, g_server_private_key.public_key, 32);
2257         log_debug("Using server identity key for ACDS session");
2258       } else {
2259         // No identity key available - use zero key
2260         // ACDS will accept this if identity verification is not required
2261         memset(create_params.identity_pubkey, 0, 32);
2262         log_debug("No server identity key - using zero key for ACDS session");
2263       }
2264 
2265       create_params.capabilities = 0x03; // Video + Audio
2266       create_params.max_participants = GET_OPTION(max_clients);
2267       log_debug("ACDS: max_clients option value = %d", GET_OPTION(max_clients));
2268 
2269       // Set password if configured
2270       create_params.has_password = has_password;
2271       if (has_password) {
2272         // TODO: Hash password with Argon2id
2273         SAFE_STRNCPY(create_params.password, password, sizeof(create_params.password));
2274       }
2275 
2276       // Set IP disclosure policy (determined above)
2277       create_params.acds_expose_ip = acds_expose_ip_flag;
2278       log_info("DEBUG: Server setting acds_expose_ip=%d (explicit_expose=%d, has_password=%d, has_identity=%d)",
2279                create_params.acds_expose_ip, explicit_expose, has_password, has_identity);
2280 
2281       // Set session type (Direct TCP or WebRTC)
2282       // Auto-detect: Use WebRTC if UPnP failed OR if explicitly requested via --webrtc
2283       // Exception: If bind address is 0.0.0.0, server is on public IP - use Direct TCP
2284       const char *bind_addr = GET_OPTION(address);
2285       bool bind_all_interfaces = (strcmp(bind_addr, "0.0.0.0") == 0);
2286 
2287       // Determine session type: prefer WebRTC by default (unless explicitly disabled)
2288       // Priority: explicit --webrtc flag > connection type detection > UPnP > default
2289       if (GET_OPTION(webrtc)) {
2290         // Explicit WebRTC request
2291         create_params.session_type = SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC;
2292         log_info("ACDS session type: WebRTC (explicitly requested via --webrtc)");
2293       } else if (bind_all_interfaces) {
2294         // Bind to 0.0.0.0: use WebRTC as default (better NAT compatibility)
2295         create_params.session_type = SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC;
2296         log_info("ACDS session type: WebRTC (default for 0.0.0.0 binding, provides NAT-agnostic connections)");
2297       } else if (upnp_succeeded) {
2298         // UPnP port mapping worked - can use direct TCP
2299         create_params.session_type = SESSION_TYPE_DIRECT_TCP;
2300         log_info("ACDS session type: Direct TCP (UPnP succeeded, server is publicly accessible)");
2301       } else {
2302         // UPnP failed and not on public IP - use WebRTC for NAT traversal
2303         create_params.session_type = SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC;
2304         log_info("ACDS session type: WebRTC (UPnP failed, server behind NAT)");
2305       }
2306 
2307       // Server connection information (where clients should connect)
2308       // If bind address is 0.0.0.0, leave server_address empty for ACDS to auto-detect public IP
2309       if (bind_all_interfaces) {
2310         create_params.server_address[0] = '\0'; // Empty - ACDS will use connection source IP
2311         log_debug("Bind address is 0.0.0.0, ACDS will auto-detect public IP from connection");
2312       } else {
2313         SAFE_STRNCPY(create_params.server_address, bind_addr, sizeof(create_params.server_address));
2314       }
2315       create_params.server_port = port;
2316 
2317       // DEBUG: Log what we're sending to ACDS
2318       log_info("DEBUG: Before SESSION_CREATE - expose_ip_publicly=%d, server_address='%s' port=%u, session_type=%u",
2319                create_params.acds_expose_ip, create_params.server_address, create_params.server_port,
2320                create_params.session_type);
2321 
2322       // Create session
2323       acds_session_create_result_t create_result;
2324       asciichat_error_t create_err = acds_session_create(g_acds_client, &create_params, &create_result);
2325 
2326       if (create_err == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2327         SAFE_STRNCPY(session_string, create_result.session_string, sizeof(session_string));
2328         SAFE_STRNCPY(g_session_string, create_result.session_string, sizeof(g_session_string));
2329         session_is_mdns_only = false; // Session is now registered with ACDS (globally discoverable)
2330         log_info("Session created: %s", session_string);
2331 
2332         // Server must join its own session so ACDS can route signaling messages
2333         log_debug("Server joining session as first participant for WebRTC signaling...");
2334         acds_session_join_params_t join_params = {0};
2335         join_params.session_string = session_string;
2336 
2337         // Use same identity key as session creation
2338         memcpy(join_params.identity_pubkey, create_params.identity_pubkey, 32);
2339 
2340         // Include password if session is password-protected
2341         if (has_password) {
2342           join_params.has_password = true;
2343           SAFE_STRNCPY(join_params.password, password, sizeof(join_params.password));
2344         }
2345 
2346         acds_session_join_result_t join_result = {0};
2347         asciichat_error_t join_err = acds_session_join(g_acds_client, &join_params, &join_result);
2348         if (join_err != ASCIICHAT_OK || !join_result.success) {
2349           log_error("Failed to join own session: %s (error: %s)", asciichat_error_string(join_err),
2350                     join_result.error_message[0] ? join_result.error_message : "unknown");
2351           // Continue anyway - this is not fatal for Direct TCP sessions
2352         } else {
2353           log_debug("Server joined session successfully (participant_id: %02x%02x...)", join_result.participant_id[0],
2354                     join_result.participant_id[1]);
2355           // Store participant ID for WebRTC signaling (needed to identify server in SDP/ICE messages)
2356           memcpy(g_server_participant_id, join_result.participant_id, 16);
2357           log_debug("Stored server participant_id for signaling: %02x%02x...", g_server_participant_id[0],
2358                     g_server_participant_id[1]);
2359           memcpy(create_result.session_id, join_result.session_id, 16);
2360         }
2361 
2362         // Keep ACDS connection alive for WebRTC signaling relay
2363         log_debug("Server staying connected to ACDS for signaling relay");
2364 
2365         // Create ACDS transport wrapper for sending signaling packets
2366         g_acds_transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("transport_acds_server", g_acds_client->socket, NULL);
2367         if (!g_acds_transport) {
2368           log_error("Failed to create ACDS transport wrapper");
2369         } else {
2370           log_debug("ACDS transport wrapper created for signaling");
2371 
2372           // Start ACDS ping thread to keep connection alive (for ALL session types)
2373           int ping_thread_result = asciichat_thread_create(&g_acds_ping_thread, "acds_ping", acds_ping_thread, NULL);
2374           if (ping_thread_result != 0) {
2375             log_error("Failed to create ACDS ping thread: %d", ping_thread_result);
2376           } else {
2377             log_debug("ACDS ping thread started to keep connection alive");
2378             g_acds_ping_thread_started = true;
2379           }
2380         }
2381 
2382         // Initialize WebRTC peer_manager if session type is WebRTC
2383         if (create_params.session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
2384           log_debug("Initializing WebRTC library and peer manager for session (role=CREATOR)...");
2385 
2386           // Initialize WebRTC library (libdatachannel)
2387           asciichat_error_t webrtc_init_result = webrtc_init();
2388           if (webrtc_init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2389             log_error("Failed to initialize WebRTC library: %s", asciichat_error_string(webrtc_init_result));
2390             g_webrtc_peer_manager = NULL;
2391           } else {
2392             log_debug("WebRTC library initialized successfully");
2393 
2394             // Configure STUN servers for ICE gathering (static to persist for peer_manager lifetime)
2395             static stun_server_t stun_servers[4] = {0};
2396             static lifecycle_t g_stun_lc = LIFECYCLE_INIT;
2397             static int stun_count = 0; // Store actual count separately
2398 
2399             if (lifecycle_init(&g_stun_lc, "stun")) {
2400               log_debug("Parsing STUN servers from options: '%s'", GET_OPTION(stun_servers));
2401               int count =
2402                   stun_servers_parse(GET_OPTION(stun_servers), OPT_ENDPOINT_STUN_SERVERS_DEFAULT, stun_servers, 4);
2403               if (count > 0) {
2404                 stun_count = count;
2405                 log_debug("Parsed %d STUN servers", count);
2406                 for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
2407                   log_debug("  STUN[%d]: '%s' (len=%d)", i, stun_servers[i].host, stun_servers[i].host_len);
2408                 }
2409               } else {
2410                 log_warn("Failed to parse STUN servers, using defaults");
2411                 stun_count = stun_servers_parse(OPT_ENDPOINT_STUN_SERVERS_DEFAULT, OPT_ENDPOINT_STUN_SERVERS_DEFAULT,
2412                                                 stun_servers, 4);
2413                 log_debug("Using default STUN servers, count=%d", stun_count);
2414                 for (int i = 0; i < stun_count; i++) {
2415                   log_debug("  STUN[%d]: '%s' (len=%d)", i, stun_servers[i].host, stun_servers[i].host_len);
2416                 }
2417               }
2418             }
2419 
2420             // Configure peer_manager
2421             webrtc_peer_manager_config_t pm_config = {
2422                 .role = WEBRTC_ROLE_CREATOR, // Server accepts offers, generates answers
2423                 .stun_servers = stun_servers,
2424                 .stun_count = stun_count,
2425                 .turn_servers = NULL, // No TURN for server (clients should have public IP or use TURN)
2426                 .turn_count = 0,
2427                 .on_transport_ready = on_webrtc_transport_ready,
2428                 .user_data = &server_ctx,
2429                 .crypto_ctx = NULL // WebRTC handles crypto internally
2430             };
2431 
2432             // Configure signaling callbacks for relaying SDP/ICE via ACDS
2433             webrtc_signaling_callbacks_t signaling_callbacks = {
2434                 .send_sdp = server_send_sdp, .send_ice = server_send_ice, .user_data = NULL};
2435 
2436             // Create peer_manager
2437             asciichat_error_t pm_result =
2438                 webrtc_peer_manager_create(&pm_config, &signaling_callbacks, &g_webrtc_peer_manager);
2439             if (pm_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2440               log_error("Failed to create WebRTC peer_manager: %s", asciichat_error_string(pm_result));
2441               g_webrtc_peer_manager = NULL;
2442             } else {
2443               log_debug("WebRTC peer_manager initialized successfully");
2444 
2445               // Start ACDS receive thread for WebRTC signaling relay
2446               int thread_result =
2447                   asciichat_thread_create(&g_acds_receive_thread, "acds_recv", acds_receive_thread, NULL);
2448               if (thread_result != 0) {
2449                 log_error("Failed to create ACDS receive thread: %d", thread_result);
2450                 // Cleanup peer_manager since signaling won't work
2451                 webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(g_webrtc_peer_manager);
2452                 g_webrtc_peer_manager = NULL;
2453               } else {
2454                 log_debug("ACDS receive thread started for WebRTC signaling relay");
2455                 g_acds_receive_thread_started = true;
2456               }
2457             }
2458           } // Close else block from webrtc_init() success
2459         } else {
2460           log_debug("Session type is DIRECT_TCP, skipping WebRTC peer_manager initialization");
2461         }
2462 
2463         // Advertise mDNS with ACDS session string
2464         // This ensures both mDNS and ACDS discovery return the same session string
2465         advertise_mdns_with_session(session_string, (uint16_t)port);
2466       } else {
2467         log_warn("Failed to create session on ACDS server (server will run without discovery)");
2468         // Clean up failed ACDS client
2469         if (g_acds_client) {
2470           acds_client_disconnect(g_acds_client);
2471           SAFE_FREE(g_acds_client);
2472           g_acds_client = NULL;
2473         }
2474       }
2475     } else {
2476       log_warn("Could not connect to ACDS server at %s:%d (server will run without discovery)", acds_server, acds_port);
2477     }
2478   } else {
2479     log_info("ACDS registration disabled (use --acds to enable)");
2480   }
2481 
2482 skip_acds_session:
2483   // Fallback: If no session string was set by ACDS (either disabled or failed),
2484   // generate a random session string for mDNS discovery only
2485   if (session_string[0] == '\0' && g_mdns_ctx) {
2486     log_debug("No ACDS session string available, generating random session for mDNS");
2487 
2488     // Use the proper session string generation from discovery module
2489     // This generates adjective-noun-noun format using the full wordlists
2490     if (acds_string_generate(session_string, sizeof(session_string)) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2491       log_error("Failed to generate session string for mDNS");
2492       return 1;
2493     }
2494 
2495     log_debug("Generated random session string for mDNS: '%s'", session_string);
2496 
2497     // Mark that this session is mDNS-only (not globally discoverable via ACDS)
2498     session_is_mdns_only = true;
2499 
2500     // Advertise mDNS with random session string
2501     advertise_mdns_with_session(session_string, (uint16_t)port);
2502   }
2503 
2504   // ====================================================================
2505   // Display session string prominently as the FINAL startup message
2506   // This ensures users see the connection info clearly without logs
2507   // wiping it away
2508   // ====================================================================
2509   if (session_string[0] != '\0') {
2510     if (session_is_mdns_only) {
2511       log_plain("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n📋 Session String: %s (LAN only via "
2512                 "mDNS)\n🔗 Share with others on your LAN to join:\n   ascii-chat "
2513                 "%s\n━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━",
2514                 session_string, session_string);
2515     } else {
2516       log_plain("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n📋 Session String: %s\n🔗 Share this "
2517                 "globally to join:\n   ascii-chat %s\n━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━",
2518                 session_string, session_string);
2519     }
2520   }
2521 
2522   // Copy session info to globals for status screen display
2523   SAFE_STRNCPY(g_session_string, session_string, sizeof(g_session_string));
2524   g_session_is_mdns_only = session_is_mdns_only;
2525 
2526   log_debug("Server entering accept loop (port %d)...", port);
2527 
2528   // Initialize status screen
2529   g_server_start_time = time(NULL);
2530   g_last_status_update = platform_get_monotonic_time_us();
2531 
2532   // Clear status screen log buffer to discard initialization logs
2533   // This ensures only NEW logs (generated after status screen starts) are displayed
2534   if (GET_OPTION(status_screen)) {
2535     ui_status_log_clear();
2536   }
2537 
2538   // Start status screen thread if enabled
2539   // Runs independently at target FPS (default 60 Hz), decoupled from network accept loop
2540   if (GET_OPTION(status_screen)) {
2541     if (asciichat_thread_create(&g_status_screen_thread, "status_screen", status_screen_thread, NULL) != 0) {
2542       log_error("Failed to create status screen thread");
2543       goto cleanup;
2544     }
2545     log_debug("Status screen thread started");
2546   }
2547 
2548   // Start WebSocket server thread if initialized
2549   if (g_websocket_server.context != NULL) {
2550     if (asciichat_thread_create(&g_websocket_server_thread, "ws_server", websocket_server_thread_wrapper,
2551                                 &g_websocket_server) != 0) {
2552       log_error("Failed to create WebSocket server thread");
2553     } else {
2554       g_websocket_server_thread_started = true;
2555       log_info("WebSocket server thread started");
2556     }
2557   }
2558 
2559   // Run TCP server (blocks until shutdown signal received)
2560   // tcp_server_run() handles:
2561   // - select() on IPv4/IPv6 sockets with timeout
2562   // - accept() new connections
2563   // - Spawn ascii_chat_client_handler() thread for each connection
2564   // - Responsive shutdown when atomic_store_bool(&g_tcp_server.running, false)
2565   asciichat_error_t run_result = tcp_server_run(&g_tcp_server);
2566   if (run_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2567     log_error("TCP server exited with error");
2568   }
2569 
2570   log_debug("Server accept loop exited");
2571 
2572 cleanup:
2573   // Signal status screen thread to exit
2574   atomic_store_bool(&g_should_exit, true);
2575 
2576   // Wait for status screen thread to finish if it was started
2577   if (GET_OPTION(status_screen)) {
2578     log_debug("Waiting for status screen thread to exit...");
2579     int join_result = asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&g_status_screen_thread, NULL, NS_PER_SEC_INT); // 1 second
2580     if (join_result != 0) {
2581       log_warn("Status screen thread did not exit cleanly (timeout)");
2582     } else {
2583       log_debug("Status screen thread exited");
2584     }
2585   }
2586 
2587   // Cleanup
2588   log_debug("Server shutting down...");
2589   memset(g_session_string, 0, sizeof(g_session_string)); // Clear session string for status screen
2590 
2591   // Wake up any threads that might be blocked on condition variables
2592   // (like packet queues) to ensure responsive shutdown
2593   // This must happen BEFORE client cleanup to wake up any blocked threads
2594   static_cond_broadcast(&g_shutdown_cond);
2595   // NOTE: Do NOT call cond_destroy() on statically-initialized condition variables
2596   // g_shutdown_cond uses STATIC_COND_INIT which doesn't allocate resources that need cleanup
2597   // Calling cond_destroy() on a static cond is undefined behavior on some platforms
2598 
2599   // Close all client sockets immediately to unblock receive threads.
2600   // The signal handler only closed the listening socket, but client receive threads
2601   // are still blocked in recv_with_timeout(). We need to close their sockets to unblock them.
2602   log_debug("Closing all client sockets to unblock receive threads...");
2603 
2604   // Use write lock since we're modifying client->socket
2605   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2606   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
2607     client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
2608     if (client->client_id[0] != '\0' && client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2609       socket_close(client->socket);
2610       client->socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
2611     }
2612   }
2613   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2614 
2615   log_debug("Signaling all clients to stop (sockets closed, g_should_exit set)...");
2616 
2617   // Stop and destroy server worker thread pool (stats logger, etc.)
2618   if (g_server_worker_pool) {
2619     thread_pool_destroy(g_server_worker_pool);
2620     g_server_worker_pool = NULL;
2621     log_debug("Server worker thread pool stopped");
2622   }
2623 
2624   // Destroy rate limiter
2625   if (g_rate_limiter) {
2626     rate_limiter_destroy(g_rate_limiter);
2627     g_rate_limiter = NULL;
2628   }
2629 
2630   // Shutdown WebSocket server BEFORE removing clients.
2631   // The LWS event thread runs callbacks that access transport data (impl_data).
2632   // If we destroy transports via remove_client while LWS is still running,
2633   // callbacks access freed memory (heap-use-after-free).
2634   // lws_context_destroy fires LWS_CALLBACK_CLOSED for all connections, which
2635   // closes transports and NULLs the per-session conn_data->transport pointer.
2636   // After this, remove_client can safely destroy the transport objects.
2637   bool websocket_exited_cleanly = true;  // Default to clean unless timeout detected
2638   if (g_websocket_server.context != NULL) {
2639     log_debug("Shutting down WebSocket server before client cleanup");
2640     atomic_store_u64(&g_websocket_server.running, false);
2641     // Wake the LWS event loop immediately instead of waiting for the 50ms
2642     // lws_service timeout. This is safe because the event loop thread can't
2643     // destroy the context until after it exits its loop (which requires
2644     // seeing running=false, which we just set).
2645     websocket_server_cancel_service(&g_websocket_server);
2646 
2647     // Join with 500ms timeout. If the WebSocket thread is deadlocked in lws_service(),
2648     // it won't exit and we skip the graceful handler thread cleanup to avoid prolonged
2649     // shutdown hangs. Forceful cleanup will happen in websocket_server_destroy().
2650     if (g_websocket_server_thread_started) {
2651       int join_result =
2652           asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&g_websocket_server_thread, NULL, 500 * NS_PER_MS_INT); // 500ms in ns
2653       if (join_result != 0) {
2654         log_warn("WebSocket thread did not exit within 500ms, skipping graceful handler cleanup");
2655         websocket_exited_cleanly = false;
2656       }
2657       g_websocket_server_thread_started = false;
2658     }
2659 
2660     if (websocket_exited_cleanly) {
2661       log_debug("WebSocket thread confirmed exited, waiting for handler threads to complete...");
2662     }
2663   }
2664 
2665   // Wait for all client handler threads to exit BEFORE destroying WebSocket context
2666   // Handler threads may still be working with WebSocket connections, so we must
2667   // ensure they complete before destroying the context (which would abort their operations).
2668   // Skip this if WebSocket thread didn't exit cleanly (deadlock detected).
2669   if (websocket_exited_cleanly) {
2670     int retry_count = 0;
2671     while (retry_count < 50) { // Max 500ms wait
2672       rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2673       bool all_cleaned = true;
2674       for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
2675         if (g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] != '\0') {
2676           all_cleaned = false;
2677           break;
2678         }
2679       }
2680       rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2681 
2682       if (all_cleaned)
2683         break;
2684       platform_sleep_us(10 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 10ms
2685       retry_count++;
2686     }
2687     log_debug("Client handler threads cleaned up, destroying WebSocket server");
2688   } else {
2689     log_warn("Skipping graceful handler thread cleanup due to WebSocket deadlock");
2690   }
2691 
2692   // ★ CRITICAL: Cleanup debug sync BEFORE destroying websocket_server
2693   // The debug_sync thread runs concurrently and accesses the named registry.
2694   // If websocket_server_destroy() frees thread pool memory first, the debug thread
2695   // will get a use-after-free error trying to check condition variables.
2696   // Moving this before websocket destroy prevents the race condition.
2697 #ifndef NDEBUG
2698   debug_sync_destroy();
2699 #endif
2700 
2701   // Now destroy WebSocket server after all handler threads have completed
2702   // This ensures no handler threads are still accessing the context.
2703   // (It's safe to call even if context was already destroyed forcefully above)
2704   websocket_server_destroy(&g_websocket_server);
2705   log_debug("WebSocket server destroyed");
2706 
2707   // Cleanup status screen log capture (must be AFTER all client threads exit to avoid use-after-free)
2708   // Client threads may still be appending logs during the wait loop above, so we destroy the buffer only
2709   // after we've confirmed they've all exited.
2710   ui_status_log_destroy();
2711 
2712   // Clean up all connected clients
2713   log_debug("Cleaning up connected clients...");
2714   // FIXED: Simplified to collect client IDs first, then remove them without holding locks
2715   char clients_to_remove[MAX_CLIENTS][MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN];
2716   int client_count = 0;
2717 
2718   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2719   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
2720     client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
2721 
2722     // Only attempt to clean up clients that were actually connected
2723     // (client_id is empty string for uninitialized clients)
2724     // FIXED: Only access mutex for initialized clients to avoid accessing uninitialized mutex
2725     if (client->client_id[0] == '\0') {
2726       continue; // Skip uninitialized clients
2727     }
2728 
2729     // Use snapshot pattern to avoid holding both locks simultaneously
2730     // This prevents deadlock by not acquiring client_state_mutex while holding rwlock
2731     SAFE_STRNCPY(clients_to_remove[client_count], client->client_id, MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN - 1);
2732 
2733     // Clean up ANY client that was allocated, whether active or not
2734     // (disconnected clients may not be active but still have resources)
2735     client_count++;
2736   }
2737   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2738 
2739   // Remove all clients without holding any locks
2740   for (int i = 0; i < client_count; i++) {
2741     if (remove_client(&server_ctx, clients_to_remove[i]) != 0) {
2742       log_error("Failed to remove client %s during shutdown", clients_to_remove[i]);
2743     }
2744   }
2745 
2746   // Clean up hash table
2747   // Cannot use HASH_ITER when deleting - HASH_DELETE modifies the table and can set
2748   // the pointer to NULL, causing HASH_ITER to dereference a null pointer.
2749   // Instead, repeatedly delete the first item until the table is empty.
2750   while (g_client_manager.clients_by_id) {
2751     client_info_t *current_client = g_client_manager.clients_by_id;
2752     HASH_DELETE(hh, g_client_manager.clients_by_id, current_client);
2753     // Note: We don't free current_client here because it's part of the clients[] array
2754   }
2755 
2756   // Clean up audio mixer
2757   if (g_audio_mixer) {
2758     // Set to NULL first before destroying.
2759     // Client handler threads may still be running and checking g_audio_mixer.
2760     // Setting it to NULL first prevents use-after-free race condition.
2761     // volatile ensures this write is visible to other threads immediately
2762     mixer_t *mixer_to_destroy = g_audio_mixer;
2763     g_audio_mixer = NULL;
2764     mixer_destroy(mixer_to_destroy);
2765   }
2766 
2767   // Clean up H.265 codec server context
2768   if (g_h265_server) {
2769     // Set to NULL first before destroying, similar to g_audio_mixer
2770     // Client handler threads may still be running and checking g_h265_server.
2771     h265_server_context_t *h265_to_destroy = g_h265_server;
2772     g_h265_server = NULL;
2773     h265_server_context_destroy(h265_to_destroy);
2774     log_debug("H.265 codec server context shut down");
2775   }
2776 
2777   // Clean up mDNS context
2778   if (g_mdns_ctx) {
2779     asciichat_mdns_destroy(g_mdns_ctx);
2780     g_mdns_ctx = NULL;
2781     log_debug("mDNS context shut down");
2782   }
2783 
2784   // Clean up synchronization primitives
2785   rwlock_destroy(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2786   mutex_destroy(&g_client_manager.mutex);
2787 
2788 #ifndef NDEBUG
2789   // Clean up statistics system
2790   stats_cleanup();
2791 
2792   // Note: debug_sync_destroy() is now called earlier (before websocket_server_destroy)
2793   // to avoid use-after-free race condition with the debug monitoring thread
2794 #endif
2795 
2796   // Destroy session host (before TCP server shutdown)
2797   if (server_ctx.session_host) {
2798     log_debug("Destroying session host");
2799     session_host_destroy(server_ctx.session_host);
2800     server_ctx.session_host = NULL;
2801   }
2802 
2803   // Destroy H.265 server codec context
2804   if (server_ctx.h265_server) {
2805     log_debug("Destroying H.265 server context");
2806     h265_server_context_destroy(server_ctx.h265_server);
2807     server_ctx.h265_server = NULL;
2808   }
2809 
2810   // Shutdown TCP server (closes listen sockets and cleans up)
2811   tcp_server_destroy(&g_tcp_server);
2812 
2813   // WebSocket server already shut down before client cleanup (above).
2814 
2815   // Join ACDS threads (if started)
2816   // NOTE: Must be done BEFORE destroying transport to ensure clean shutdown
2817   if (g_acds_ping_thread_started) {
2818     log_debug("Joining ACDS ping thread");
2819     asciichat_thread_join(&g_acds_ping_thread, NULL);
2820     g_acds_ping_thread_started = false;
2821     log_debug("ACDS ping thread joined");
2822   }
2823 
2824   if (g_acds_receive_thread_started) {
2825     log_debug("Joining ACDS receive thread");
2826     asciichat_thread_join(&g_acds_receive_thread, NULL);
2827     g_acds_receive_thread_started = false;
2828     log_debug("ACDS receive thread joined");
2829   }
2830 
2831   // Clean up WebRTC peer manager (if initialized for ACDS signaling relay)
2832   if (g_webrtc_peer_manager) {
2833     log_debug("Destroying WebRTC peer manager");
2834     webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(g_webrtc_peer_manager);
2835     g_webrtc_peer_manager = NULL;
2836   }
2837 
2838   // Clean up ACDS transport wrapper (if created)
2839   if (g_acds_transport) {
2840     log_debug("Destroying ACDS transport wrapper");
2841     acip_transport_destroy(g_acds_transport);
2842     g_acds_transport = NULL;
2843   }
2844 
2845   // Disconnect from ACDS server (if connected for WebRTC signaling relay)
2846   if (g_acds_client) {
2847     log_debug("Disconnecting from ACDS server");
2848     acds_client_disconnect(g_acds_client);
2849     SAFE_FREE(g_acds_client);
2850     g_acds_client = NULL;
2851   }
2852   // Disable keepawake mode (re-allow OS to sleep)
2853   platform_disable_keepawake();
2854 
2855   // Clean up platform-specific resources (Windows: Winsock cleanup, timer restoration)
2856   // POSIX: minimal cleanup (symbol cache already handled above on Windows)
2857   socket_cleanup();
2858 
2859   log_info("Server shutdown complete");
2860 
2861   asciichat_error_stats_print();
2862 
2863   // Use exit() to allow atexit() handlers to run
2864   // Cleanup functions are idempotent (check if initialized first)
2865   return 0;
2866 }

Variable
  Documentation




mixer_t* volatile g_audio_mixer = NULL


Global audio mixer instance for multi-client audio processing.
    Global audio mixer.




The mixer combines audio streams from multiple clients, excluding
    each client's own audio from their outbound stream (preventing echo).
    Created once during server initialization and shared by all audio render
    threads.




THREAD SAFETY: The mixer itself is thread-safe and can be used
    concurrently by multiple render.c audio threads without external
    synchronization. During shutdown, set to NULL before destroying to prevent
    use-after-free.


Definition at line 173 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by add_client(), add_webrtc_client(),
    client_audio_render_thread(), remove_client(), and
    server_main().






public_key_t g_client_whitelist[MAX_CLIENTS] =
  {0}


Global client public key whitelist. Whitelist of allowed client
    public keys.




Array of public keys for clients that are authorized to connect to
    the server. Used for client authentication when whitelist mode is enabled.
    Sized to hold up to MAX_CLIENTS entries.




Note


Only used when client authentication is enabled



Accessed from crypto.c for client authentication




Definition at line 523 of file server/main.c.


523 {0};






Referenced by server_crypto_handshake(), and
    server_main().






h265_server_context_t* volatile g_h265_server = NULL


Global H.265 codec server context. Shared H.265 decoding context
    used by all packet handlers to decode client H.265 frames. Each client gets
    its own decoder instance.




THREAD SAFETY: The h265_server context is thread-safe and can be
    used concurrently by multiple client handlers. Decoders are per-client.
    During shutdown, set to NULL before destroying to prevent
  use-after-free.


Definition at line 185 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by handle_image_frame_h265_packet(), and
    server_main().






size_t
  g_num_server_identity_keys = 0


Number of loaded server identity keys. Number of server identity
    keys in use.




Tracks how many identity keys were successfully loaded from --key
    flags. Zero means server is running in simple mode (no identity key).


Definition at line 510 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by server_crypto_handshake().






size_t
  g_num_whitelisted_clients = 0


Number of whitelisted clients. Tracks the current number of
    entries in g_client_whitelist that are valid and active. Used to iterate the
    whitelist and check authorization.


Definition at line 533 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by server_crypto_handshake(), and
    server_main().






rate_limiter_t* g_rate_limiter = NULL


Global rate limiter for connection attempts and packet processing.
    Global connection rate limiter.




In-memory rate limiter to prevent connection flooding and DoS
    attacks. Tracks connection attempts and packet rates per IP address with
    configurable limits.




Default limits (from rate_limit.c):


    
  
  • RATE_EVENT_CONNECTION: 50 connections per 60 seconds
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_IMAGE_FRAME: 144 FPS (8640 frames/min)
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_AUDIO: 172 FPS (10320 packets/min)
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_PING: 2 Hz (120 pings/min)
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_CLIENT_JOIN: 10 joins per 60 seconds
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_CONTROL: 100 control packets per 60 seconds
    • 



THREAD SAFETY: The rate limiter is thread-safe and can be used
    concurrently from the main accept loop and packet handlers without external
    synchronization.




See also


main.h for extern declaration


Definition at line 215 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by process_decrypted_packet(), and
    server_main().






bool g_server_encryption_enabled = false


Global flag indicating if server encryption is enabled. Server
    encryption enabled flag.




Set to true when the server is configured to use encryption and
    has successfully loaded a private key. Controls whether the server performs
    cryptographic handshakes with clients.




Note


Accessed from crypto.c for server-side crypto
  operations


Definition at line 474 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by server_crypto_handshake(), and

private_key_t
  g_server_identity_keys[MAX_IDENTITY_KEYS] = {0}


Global server identity keys array (multi-key support). Array of
    server identity keys.




Stores all server identity keys loaded from multiple --key flags.
    Enables servers to present different keys (SSH, GPG) based on client
    expectations. Server selects the appropriate key during handshake based on
    what the client downloaded from ACDS.




Note


g_server_private_key points to g_server_identity_keys[0]
  for compatibility


Definition at line 500 of file server/main.c.


500 {0};






Referenced by server_crypto_handshake().






private_key_t g_server_private_key = {0}


Global server private key (first identity key, for backward
    compatibility). Server's private key.




Stores the server's primary private key loaded from the first
    --key flag. Used for cryptographic handshakes and packet
    encryption/decryption. Initialized during server startup from the configured
    key file path.




Note


This is an alias to g_server_identity_keys[0] for
  backward compatibility



Accessed from crypto.c for server-side crypto operations

static_cond_t g_shutdown_cond =
  STATIC_COND_INIT


Global shutdown condition variable for waking blocked threads.
    Used to wake up threads that might be blocked on condition variables (like
    packet queues) during shutdown. This ensures responsive shutdown even when
    threads are waiting on blocking operations.
